evidence_cited.claim: spec.md vision did not authorize Phase 2 scope
F17-F19 are NEW scope, not gap closure

description: Spec organization: amend spec.md with Phase 1 / Phase 2 sections AND create .harness/features.json (user-approved Option (c) of the three offered). Resolves architect A1 (spec scope drift) and scope-guardian section 4 (features.json baseline gap) in a single coherent change.

evidence_cited.claim: rubric naming collision risk between eval-harness and harness-build

description: Rubric naming: keep eval-harness (meta layer) and harness-build (runtime layer) names; ship decision guide in Sprint 11 via rubrics/README.md (user-approved Option (a)). Architect A2 alternatives (rename to eval-methodology, or two-level namespace) deferred. Watchpoint: revisit if Sprint 11 dogfood findings show user confusion.

evidence_cited.claim: permanent examples/ surface area unauthorized by spec; maintenance obligation
Sprint 10 as planned exceeds single-context-window budget

description: Sprint 10 dogfood: ephemeral tmp-directory verification, NO permanent examples/ commit (user-approved Option (a)). Findings persist as .harness/dogfood-findings.md only. Phase 2 Technical Constraints amended to declare this. Should-NOT gate added to Sprint 10 contract requirement: examples/ directory must not be created in this repo.

evidence_cited.claim: sprint-state.json showed current_sprint:6 but no Sprint 6 entry; eval never executed
Sprint 6 evaluation round 1: PASS 100% (11/11 criteria, all gates pass, Context Engineering 5/5)

description: Sprint 6 closure: invoked trine-eval:evaluator subagent directly (sprint-workflow skill disable-model-invocation blocked /harness-sprint auto-invocation). Verdict PASS 100%. sprint-state.json updated to add Sprint 6 entry + advance current_sprint to 7. This is a procedural deviation from the standard /harness-sprint flow — recorded for retrospective review.

evidence_cited.claim: Sprint 7's conditional hard threshold has no rubric-format precedent
templates/by-rubric/ pre-empts future per-rubric template proliferation
playbook_stage field scoping prevents schema bleed to other project types
Should-NOT gates required to protect prior-deliverable surfaces

description: Phase 2 mechanical fixes folded into sprints 7-11: (1) Sprint 7 sandboxing threshold made unconditional; (2) Sprint 7 contract MUST include Should-NOT gates protecting Sprint 6 JIT annotations and existing 5-rubric Available Rubrics list; (3) Sprint 8 uses templates/by-rubric/harness-build.json layout; (4) Sprint 9 scopes playbook_stage field to harness-build mode and adds planner.md backward-compat Should-NOT gate; (5) Sprint 11 plan unchanged. Each sprint's 'protects_prior' and 'notes' fields encode the constraints for Generator pickup at /harness-sprint NN invocation.
